findings.feature: Perforated intake band height (front elevation)
findings.evidence: Ref (apple_front): band spans y820-905 of a 655px total height = 0.13 H (~12.5mm real), 8-10 hole rows clearly exposed. Render front: band spans ~y1020-1075 of 850px = 0.065 H, roughly half the reference height, with only ~5-7 rows readable (detail frame shows ~7 before wrap-under). Camera foreshortening in the render is mild (near-level) and cannot account for a 2x deficit.
findings.fix_hint: Raise the exposed perf zone to ~12-13mm of front elevation (0.13 of total front height incl. base): extend perforation rows upward onto the bottom fillet so 9-10 rows read in a head-on view before the base reveal.

findings.feature: Footprint corner radius at top silhouette (plan-view superellipse corner seen from slightly above)
findings.evidence: Render front: top-left silhouette runs vertical edge, then a STRAIGHT diagonal facet ~(255,335)->(425,268), then the top edge; mirrored facet at top-right. Refs (apple_front, wikimedia): the corner silhouette is a continuous smooth quarter-curve, no straight segment at any viewing angle.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the chamfer/low-segment blend with a smooth constant-radius plan corner ~26mm, tangent-continuous (G2 if possible) into both faces; >=24 segments or subdivision so no facet line survives at 4K.

findings.feature: Power LED dot position
findings.evidence: Ref: LED center is 0.172 W from the right edge (~34mm), on the port-row centerline (y745 vs ports y730), dia ~0.008 W. Render front: dot at x1712 with right edge at 1780 = 0.044 W (~8.7mm) from the corner, and ~33px ABOVE the port centerline (y845 vs y878). Perspective compresses the render's right side but not by 4x; the dot is hugging the corner.
findings.fix_hint: Move LED to 34mm from the front-right vertical corner, vertically centered on the port/SD row centerline; keep dia ~1.6mm. (Its dark-pit read is emission/material, but verify it is a flush surface feature, not a drilled hole.)

findings.feature: USB-C pocket width / stadium aspect
findings.evidence: Ref: vertical stadium ~13x55px = 0.0101 W wide, aspect ~4.2:1 (~2.0-2.5mm x 8.5mm). Render front: ~20x70px = 0.0129 W, aspect ~3.5:1; detail frame confirms 50x165px = aspect 3.3:1 and width 0.099 of SD-slot width vs 0.076 in ref. Pockets are ~25-30% too fat.
findings.fix_hint: Keep pocket height (~8.5mm) and end radii = half width, but narrow width to ~2.5mm so h:w is ~4.2:1.

findings.feature: SD slot height
findings.evidence: Ref: slot 172x14px, h/w = 0.081 (~26.4 x 2.1mm). Render front: 215x22px, h/w = 0.102; detail frame: 505x60px, h/w = 0.119. Slot is 25-45% too tall; width itself is correct (0.139 W vs ref 0.134 W).
findings.fix_hint: Hold width at ~26.5mm, reduce aperture height to ~2.1-2.2mm (h/w ~= 0.08), end radii = half height.

findings.feature: Blank fillet lip between face bottom edge and first perforation row
findings.evidence: Ref zoom (apple_front): first hole row begins ~10px below the face-bottom tangent = 0.008 W (~1.5mm); holes occupy the fillet itself. Render detail: crisp crease at y~700, first holes at y~760 = gap ~0.016 W (~3mm) of blank convex lip, and the crease reads as a hard seam the real device does not have.
findings.fix_hint: Start the first perforation row ~1.5mm below the face-plane tangent and soften the face-to-fillet transition so no hard seam line precedes the holes.

findings.feature: SD slot interior
findings.evidence: Render detail shows a bright specular strip spanning the slot interior (internal reader plane catching light just inside the aperture). All three refs show a uniformly dark aperture with only a subtle entry bevel.
findings.fix_hint: Recess the internal reader/tongue geometry >=3mm behind the aperture and add the shallow entry chamfer (~0.3mm) so the opening self-shadows.

findings.feature: Base reveal / foot shadow line under the perf band
findings.evidence: Ref: crisp dark reveal line ~0.008 W (~1.5mm) tall under the band, near-constant height across the width, lifting only at the corner wraps. Render front: only a soft gradient shadow; no defined recessed base puck line is readable.
findings.fix_hint: Model the inset circular base puck creating a ~1.5-2mm vertical reveal recessed ~4mm behind the band silhouette so a constant dark gap line reads head-on.

findings.feature: Overall W:H proportion of front elevation
findings.evidence: Ref head-on: H/W = 655/1284 = 0.51 (aluminum face alone 0.44). Render: H/W ~ 850/1552 = 0.55 (face alone 715/1552 = 0.46), i.e. ~5-8% tall, though the render's mild above-axis perspective inflates this; true error likely ~4-5%.
findings.fix_hint: Verify body dims against 197 x 197 x 95mm; if shell height exceeds 95mm-equivalent, scale Z down ~4%. Re-render one orthographic front frame to confirm.

findings.feature: Port group lateral position (first USB-C from left edge)
findings.evidence: Ref: P1 center at 0.164 W from left edge (~32mm). Render: 0.149 W, but the render camera is slightly off-axis, stretching/compressing halves by a few percent, which covers the difference.
findings.fix_hint: Confirm in an orthographic front render that P1 center sits ~32mm from the left silhouette edge; adjust only if it deviates >1mm.

findings.feature: Perforation pitch
findings.evidence: Ref: pitch/SD-slot-width = 9.5/172 = 0.055. Render detail: 27/505 = 0.053 (~1.45mm real pitch). Hex packing and hole/pitch ratio visually match.
findings.fix_hint: None - do not touch pitch when extending the band height.

findings.feature: Port row placement and spacing (vertical position, port-to-port, port-to-SD gap, SD width, count, vertical-stadium orientation)
findings.evidence: Row center at 0.73 of total height in both (ref 730/655-span vs render 878/850-span); port spacing 0.076 W ref vs 0.077 W render; gap/spacing ratio 0.94 in both; SD width 0.134 W ref vs 0.139 W render; two vertical stadium ports left, SD center-left, matching layout.
findings.fix_hint: None - preserve these datums while narrowing the port/slot apertures.

grades.why: Layout datums (count, orientation, spacing, row height, SD width) are reproduction-grade, but every aperture is oversized: ports ~30% too wide (aspect 3.3-3.5 vs 4.2), SD slot 25-45% too tall with a non-reference bright internal plane, and the LED is parked ~25mm too close to the right corner.

Hole pitch is an excellent match, but the band is ~half the reference height, shows too few rows, starts after a 2x-too-wide blank lip below a hard seam, and lacks the crisp base reveal beneath - the base of the object reads thin and vague instead of being the Studio's signature feature.

Face W:H within ~5% (perspective-limited measurement), port row at the correct 0.73 height, lateral rhythm of ports/SD matches; residual doubt is a probable ~4-5% excess height and slight left-bias of the port group.

Top plan-view corners show straight chamfer facets in silhouette where the reference is a smooth continuous curve at every angle - an immediate machinist-level tell; bottom fillet wrap is plausible but partly obscured.
Reads as a Mac Studio at arm's length, but the halved intake band, faceted top corners, fat port apertures, and mispositioned LED collectively fail the head-on A/B against the Apple press frame.

notes: Reference quality: apple_front.jpg is a clean orthographic-ish press frame and served as the primary datum source; wikimedia_front.jpg is elevated 3/4 (good for corner-curve and pocket-interior checks, weak for front ratios); mac-studio-front-ref.jpg is low-res and only corroborates layout. The render frames are NOT orthographic - mild above-axis, slightly off-center perspective - which caps confidence on overall W:H (+/-5%) and lateral positions (+/-2% W); the LED misposition exceeds that noise floor by ~4x so it stands. No reference resolves the exact perf row count at macro, so band height was graded on relative elevation share, not row count. LED is lit in refs and unlit in the render; geometry judgment was limited to its position/diameter. Recommend the fix loop re-render a true orthographic front at matching focal length before the next audit to remove the perspective caveats. mm conversions assume the real 197mm body width.

findings.feature: Apple logo inlay on top face
findings.evidence: Both refs show a dark centered logo (~0.25-0.28 of body width; ref2 zoom logo spans ~285px on a ~1000px top face). Horizontal scans across the q34 render top face (y=500-950, x=1000-3200) read a uniform 104-124 gradient with zero local dips; side-render top sliver likewise smooth 188-251. No logo exists on the model.
findings.fix_hint: Add flush circular logo inlay, diameter ~50mm (0.25 of the 197mm width), centered on the top face; model as a shallow material inlay (mirror finish) with a crisp boundary, not a decal glow.

findings.feature: Bottom edge / base undercut (wall-to-floor termination)
findings.evidence: Ref1 (4.42px/mm): wall holds full luminance (194-197) to within 8px (~1.8mm) of the bottom, then crashes to a black recess gap ~2-3mm tall. Render side view: left silhouette starts curling inward at y=1985 and pulls in 90px (~5.3mm) over the final 125px (~7.4mm) of height (17px/mm); luminance fades over ~40px with no discrete gap step. The base reads melted, bar-of-soap, instead of a vertical wall snapping into a hidden plinth.
findings.fix_hint: Keep side wall dead vertical until the last ~2mm of height; bottom fillet radius <=2mm; then step to a recessed black base plinth inset ~8-10mm from the wall with a ~3mm visible dark gap band and crisp shadow line.

findings.feature: Stance / height-to-width ratio
findings.evidence: Side render front-plane measurements: front-top edge y~655-676, bottom extinction y~2110-2125 -> height 1470+/-30px against front-plane width ~3330px -> h/W ~= 0.44. Real device is 95/197 = 0.482. Render is ~8% squat (+/-3% method error from shadowed bottom and corner-tangent depth).
findings.fix_hint: Set body height to 0.482 x footprint width (95mm on a 197mm square); confirm with a true orthographic front elevation where h/W must measure 0.482 exactly.

findings.feature: Footprint (plan) corner radius
findings.evidence: Two independent render measures agree: q34 tangent-ratio method (corner arc extent 510px vs front+right edge extents 1670+1110px) gives r ~= 0.134W; side-view corner-cut horizontal extents (586px near / 319px far, avg 452px on 3346px width) give r ~= 0.135W (~26.5mm). Same method on ref2 gives r ~= 0.149W (~29.5mm). Render radius runs ~10% tight, consistently in the same direction.
findings.fix_hint: Increase plan corner radius from ~26.5mm to ~29-30mm (0.15 x 197mm), keeping the corner a true vertical cylinder; verify on a top-down orthographic render against a rounded-rect template.

findings.feature: Right-side wall shoulder flare below top corner (possible bulge)
findings.evidence: Threshold trace of the side render right silhouette peaks at x=3469 (y~740, just under the corner cut) vs a mid-height waist of x=3362 (y~1460): a +3.2%W (~6mm) flare-then-pinch. However edge contrast there is only ~20 gray levels under heavy bloom, while the high-contrast left silhouette is dead straight (q34 left edge linear 205->281 over y=700-1580, residual ~1px), so this may be a bloom artifact rather than geometry.
findings.fix_hint: Re-render a matte clay orthographic side elevation (no bloom, no DoF); if the shoulder deviates >0.5mm from vertical, crease the top-fillet-to-corner-cylinder blend so the wall leaves the fillet tangent-vertical with zero overshoot.

findings.feature: Power LED position on front face
findings.evidence: Ref1: LED sits ~56% across the front face at roughly port-row height. Render q34: faint dot at org (2064,2294), ~75-84% across after first-order wide-angle correction and sitting near the bottom edge rather than the port line. Low confidence due to perspective, but both axes trend off.
findings.fix_hint: Place LED at ~0.56 x face width from the left edge, vertically on the port-row centerline; diameter ~2mm, flush.

findings.feature: Top surface flatness
findings.evidence: Side render corner-free central span (x=1950-2950): top silhouette y range 381-386px, quadratic sagitta 1.9px ~= 0.1mm at 17px/mm. Dead flat, matching the machined reference. Not domed.
findings.fix_hint: None - do not touch; keep top plane mathematically flat.

findings.feature: Top-edge fillet radius (top-to-side roll)
findings.evidence: Side render 90%->10% luminance band across the top-front edge is 36-37px at three stations = ~2.1mm at 17px/mm (upper bound, includes bloom). Ref transition is resolution-limited at 4-5px (<=2.5mm). Geometric fillet ~2mm is consistent with the reference; the soft look in q34 is specular bloom/DoF, not geometry.
findings.fix_hint: Keep fillet R ~= 2-2.5mm, constant around the full perimeter including through the plan corners; address the mushy visual read in the bloom/DoF post settings, not the mesh.

findings.feature: Front port group (2x USB-C + SD slot) presence and grouping
findings.evidence: Render q34 shows two port slots plus SD slot grouped in the left quarter of the front face, matching ref1 layout (ports at ~0.06W/0.11W, SD centered ~0.20W). Normalized SD length ~0.10-0.13 of face width in both after perspective allowance.
findings.fix_hint: None; keep grouping. Only re-verify slot lengths on an orthographic front render (SD slot ~26mm, USB-C 9x3.2mm).

grades.why: Measured sagitta ~0.1mm over the central 60% of the span - dead flat, matches reference; only withholding 10 for lack of a top-down orthographic to rule out edge-region dishing.

Geometric radius measures ~2mm (36px band at 17px/mm), consistent with the resolution-limited reference crash (4-5px). The soft appearance is bloom/DoF, not mesh. Minor uncertainty where the fillet blends into plan corners (specular dims over the blend).

Render measures r ~= 0.134-0.135W via two independent methods vs ref ~0.149W - about 10% tight, consistent direction but inside a +/-10% method band. Corner is correctly a vertical cylinder (arc extents match top and bottom).

High-contrast left silhouettes in both frames are dead straight (residual ~1px over 900+px); apparent right-side flare/waist (~3%W) is unconfirmable under bloom. Main real defect is the early inward roll in the bottom 8% of the wall.

Reads as a rounded-square extrusion, not a soft cube - flat top, straight walls, near-correct plan radius. Dragged down by the melted bottom termination (no crisp undercut/plinth step), ~8% squat stance, and the blank top face missing the logo that anchors the object's identity in every reference view.

notes: Reference quality: both refs are oblique lifestyle shots (no true front/top orthographic, moderate JPEG softness); ref fillet and bottom-gap measurements are resolution-limited (4.42px/mm), so sub-millimeter claims are bounded, not exact. Render limitations: the 'side' frame is not a true elevation - corner-cut extents of 586px (left) vs 319px (right) reveal a few degrees of yaw, and heavy bloom + DoF made the low-contrast right silhouette unverifiable; the q34 frame is a close wide-angle with strong left-to-right scale gradient, so feature-size ratios there carry ~20% uncertainty. All perspective corrections are first-order; stance (-8%) and corner-radius (-10%) findings carry roughly +/-3-5% method error but are directionally consistent across independent measurements. Strong recommendation for the next fix loop: emit three diagnostic frames - matte clay orthographic front elevation, orthographic side elevation, and top-down plan - which would settle the shoulder-flare question, the exact h/W, and the plan radius in one pass. Angles we lack: rear (power inlet, vent field) and underside (intake ring) were not auditable from either reference or render set.

findings.feature: Body width:height ratio (overall envelope)
findings.evidence: Drawing back view: 1248 px wide x 597 px tall = W/H 2.090 (matches 19.7/9.5 cm spec). Render front frame: width 2717 px (silhouette 445..3156 at mid-face), total height 1487 px (front rim crest y~598 to ground contact y~2085) = W/H 1.83. Face-only cross-check: drawing 1248/544 = 2.294 vs render 2717/1347 = 2.017. Both say the body is 13-14% too tall relative to width. Camera pitch is only ~2.3 deg (top-face strip 108 px), so foreshortening error is <0.1% - this is a real model defect, not perspective.
findings.fix_hint: Scale the model's height (Z) by x0.88: current height reads as 10.78 cm-equivalent at the render's 137.9 px/cm width scale; target is 9.5 cm total (body face 8.6 cm + base zone 0.9 cm) against the 19.7 cm width. Do not touch width/depth.

findings.feature: Rear I/O panel (4x Thunderbolt, ethernet, power inlet, 2x USB-A, HDMI, 3.5mm jack, power button) and rear vent field
findings.evidence: The back+side drawing fully dimensions the rear: port row centered 0.826 of face height from top, 4 TB ports at left, dashed vent rectangle 0.88 W wide x 0.56 face-height tall inset 22 px from top. Neither supplied render frame shows the back of the model at all.
findings.fix_hint: Add a straight-on back frame to the next audit batch; until then the entire rear panel (a third of the device's geometry) is unaudited.

findings.feature: SD card slot length
findings.evidence: Drawing: slot 178 px at 63.35 px/cm = 2.81 cm stroke-inflated, ~2.65 cm true (0.143 W). Render: 416 px at 137.9 px/cm = 3.02 cm (0.153 W). Slot is ~14% too long.
findings.fix_hint: Shorten the SD slot to 2.6-2.7 cm (0.135 W). Keep the 2.5 mm slot thickness - that measures correct (35 px).

findings.feature: SD card slot x-position
findings.evidence: Drawing: slot center 0.376 W from left edge (7.42 cm). Render: 0.401 W (7.90 cm). Slot sits ~4.9 mm too far right; both its left end (+3.6 mm) and right end (+5.7 mm) are shifted.
findings.fix_hint: Move the SD slot center left to 7.4 cm from the body's left edge (0.376 W).

findings.feature: Front USB-C port pitch (center-to-center)
findings.evidence: Drawing: centers at 0.167 W and 0.241 W = pitch 0.074 W (1.46 cm). Render: centers at 0.160 W and 0.245 W = pitch 0.085 W (1.68 cm), +15%. Port 1 is 1.4 mm too close to the left edge, port 2 is 0.9 mm too far right.
findings.fix_hint: Set USB-C centers at 3.30 cm and 4.75 cm from the left body edge (0.167 W / 0.241 W); pitch 1.46 cm.

findings.feature: Bottom chamfer / base inset (body-to-base transition)
findings.evidence: Drawing shows a distinct 0.63 cm-tall chamfer stepping in to an inset base (visible chamfer line starts 1.36 cm inboard of the body edge in elevation). In the render the contact shadow swallows everything below the perforation band (luminance <45 merges band, base and floor from y~2010 down); lateral inset could not be separated from shadow.
findings.fix_hint: Re-render one frame with a brighter floor or a low 10-15 deg camera so the base inset and chamfer profile can be measured; drawing target: base visibly inset with ~6 mm rise from ground to body bottom edge.

findings.feature: Plan squareness (depth = width) of the footprint
findings.evidence: Drawing plan is an exact square, 1252 x 1252 px. Neither render frame allows a clean depth measurement (front view hides depth; q34 depth is entangled with unknown camera pitch). q34 top face looks visually square but was not measurable to better than ~10%.
findings.fix_hint: Confirm model footprint is exactly 19.7 x 19.7 cm; add a true top-down frame if the fix loop wants proof.

findings.feature: Front port row height
findings.evidence: Drawing: port centerline 0.252 of total height above ground (0.821 of face height from top). Render: 0.268 of total height (0.808 of face from top). ~6% proportionally high; roughly 1.5 mm at true scale.
findings.fix_hint: After the Z rescale, place the USB-C/SD centerline 2.4 cm above ground (0.252 H); re-measure since the global height fix moves it.

findings.feature: Front status-LED pinhole
findings.evidence: Local-contrast search over the entire right half of the face in the front frame found no dot (max contrast blob is 3 px on the band edge). The q34 frame HAS a distinct 16x6 px dark dot but it sits low on the face near the base (orig ~x2052, y2266), not on the port centerline. Both drawings crop the right end of the front elevation, so the true position is unverifiable from these refs, but the cross-frame inconsistency means the model's LED is either absent or angle-dependent.
findings.fix_hint: Model a ~1 mm diameter pinhole on the front face near the right end (~0.94 W), on the same centerline as the USB-C ports, and confirm it reads in a straight-on frame.

findings.feature: Plan corner radius
findings.evidence: Drawing: r = 169-174 px on W 1252 = 0.135 W (2.68 cm). q34 projective measurement: tangent-to-virtual-corner 330.9 px on straight span 1661.5 px (left edge) gives r/W 0.142; right edge gives 0.146; both carry +3-6% near-end perspective bias, landing on ~0.135-0.14. Front-frame shading zone (130-170 px) is consistent with r~370 px because the steep-shading zone of a cylinder is only 0.29r. Do NOT shrink this radius.
findings.fix_hint: None - leave the corner radius alone; earlier eyeball impressions of 'too square' corners are a shading artifact, not geometry.

findings.feature: Front USB-C port opening size and aspect
findings.evidence: Render openings 124 px tall x 38 px wide = 0.90 x 0.28 cm, aspect 0.31; drawing 61 x 24 px stroke-inflated (~0.88 cm true); real USB-C receptacle ~0.9 cm. Both ports and the SD slot share one centerline in render (1687/1686/1683) exactly as drawn (937.5/938).
findings.fix_hint: None.

findings.feature: Intake band / plinth zone height
findings.evidence: Drawing plinth (body bottom edge to ground): 53/597 = 0.089 of total height. Render band+base: 140/1487 = 0.094. Proportionally within 6% - correct. Its absolute size (1.02 cm vs 0.84 cm) is inherited entirely from the global height overscale and will self-correct with the Z rescale. Perforation texture wrapping the bottom is present (band row-variance 23.5 vs 7.4 on the face), which matches the real device even though the line drawing omits holes.
findings.fix_hint: No independent change; re-verify the 0.089 H proportion after the body Z rescale.

grades.why: Drawing W/H 2.090 vs render 1.83 - the body is 13-14% too tall relative to width, confirmed independently by the face-only ratio (2.294 vs 2.017). This is the single defect that changes the object's read from 'low slab' toward 'chunky box'.

Projective measurement off the q34 top-face rim gives r/W 0.142-0.146 with a known +3-6% bias vs drawing 0.135 - a machinist-level match. Docked one point only because the measurement rests on one corner (the near one); far corners were tangent-consistent but noisier.

Counts, shapes, sizes, and shared centerline are right; USB-C centers within 1.4 mm. But the USB-C pitch is +15%, the SD slot is 4.9 mm too far right and 14% too long, and the row rides ~6% high - four small but fixable placement errors.

Proportionally the band+base zone is within 6% of the drawing's 0.089 H and the perforation ring exists. Points off because absolute size inherits the +13% height overscale and the chamfer/base-inset geometry the drawing specifies could not be verified under the contact shadow.

Everything that could be measured tracks the drawings within a few percent EXCEPT the dominant 13% height overscale, and a third of the device (rear panel, base inset, footprint squareness) is unauditable from the two supplied frames.

notes: Reference quality: the two drawings are stroke-rendered (8-11 px lines at 63.35 px/cm), so small features carry ~+/-1.5 mm uncertainty; ratios above account for stroke where it matters. The front elevation in dim_top-front is cropped at the image's right edge, so the right end of the front face (status-LED region) has no ground truth. Angles we lack: no back render frame (entire rear I/O row, power inlet, vent field unaudited despite being fully dimensioned in dim_back-side), no top-down or side frame (footprint squareness and base inset unverified). Measurement method notes: front frame is near-orthographic (camera pitch ~2.3 deg from the 108 px top-face strip; height compression <0.1%), width taper 0.4% between y900/y1200 - the 13% W:H error cannot be a perspective artifact. The q34 corner-radius numbers were derived by fitting the two straight rim edges (slopes 0.3166 / -0.5663, residuals <1 px over 1400+ px), finding tangent departure points (x~1884 and x~2426) and the virtual corner intersection (2199, 1351). One scene artifact: a small dark speck on the backdrop/floor boundary right of the body in the front frame near orig (3280, 1620) - off-body, cosmetic, worth cleaning before publishing frames. Suggested next-loop frames: straight-on back, low-angle (10-15 deg) front for the base chamfer/inset, true top-down for footprint.

findings.feature: Rear face overall elevation (flat face + proportions)
findings.evidence: No rear geometry exists in the model — zero render frames. Refs: body W:H to ground = 2.07:1 in both (photo body 1288x595px face + base; drawing 998x482px incl base = 197x95mm). Face proper is ~87-88mm tall; the bottom ~8mm is the recessed base zone.
findings.fix_hint: Build a flat rear face 197mm wide x ~87mm tall, sitting above an ~8mm recessed circular base; total height to desk 95mm. All features below mount on this plane.

findings.feature: Exhaust vent perforation field
findings.evidence: Photo: field spans x 0.057W-0.943W (88% of width, ~11.5mm side margins), y from 0.06 to 0.63 of face height (drawing dashed field agrees: 88% width, bottom at 0.61 face height). Hole dia ~10px at 0.153mm/px = ~1.5-1.7mm, staggered hex lattice, pitch ~19-20px = ~3.0mm horizontal, ~2.6mm row pitch; ~20 rows x ~57 holes = ~1,100-1,200 holes.
findings.fix_hint: Cut a 174 x 50mm perforation field, top edge 5-6mm below the top of face, centered horizontally: circular holes dia 1.6mm (add small entry chamfer ~0.2mm), hex-staggered at 3.0mm pitch, ~20 rows. Outermost 2-3 columns wrap slightly onto the corner curvature (photo shows column pitch compressing to ~0.7x at field ends).

findings.feature: Port row — count and left-to-right order
findings.evidence: Both refs agree exactly: 4x Thunderbolt (USB-C) -> RJ45 ethernet -> AC cloverleaf inlet -> 2x USB-A -> HDMI -> 3.5mm jack -> power button. 11 elements. The focus's assumed order (power before ethernet) is WRONG — ethernet at 0.379W precedes the inlet at 0.50W. Centerline at 0.80 of face height (~17mm above face bottom, ~24mm above desk).
findings.fix_hint: Lay out 11 elements on one horizontal centerline 17mm above the face bottom, at width fractions 0.158/0.206/0.254/0.302 (TB), 0.379 (RJ45), 0.500 (AC), 0.600/0.666 (USB-A), 0.75 (HDMI), 0.835 (jack), 0.905 (button).

findings.feature: Thunderbolt 4 ports x4 — VERTICAL orientation
findings.evidence: In BOTH refs the four USB-C slots are portrait pills, not landscape: photo slots ~19px wide x 65px tall = ~3.0 x 10mm; drawing ~2 x 7.5mm (drawing under-scales small cutouts). Centers at 0.158/0.206/0.254/0.302W, pitch 0.048W = ~9.5mm, refs agree within 0.002W.
findings.fix_hint: Model 4 vertical stadium slots ~3.3mm wide x 9.5mm tall (full-radius ends, recess depth ~1mm to connector face), centers at x = 31/40.5/50/59.5mm from left edge, pitch 9.5mm. Do NOT default to horizontal USB-C.

findings.feature: AC power inlet — 3-lobe cloverleaf
findings.evidence: Photo: dead center at 0.499W (drawing 0.501W), the largest port feature: recess ~135x95px = ~21 x 14.5mm, three circular lobes (2 top, 1 bottom) each dia ~48px = ~7.3mm, one metal pin per lobe dia ~12px = ~1.8mm.
findings.fix_hint: Center at x=98.5mm on the port line: cloverleaf recess ~21-23mm wide x 15-16.5mm tall, three overlapping circular bores dia ~8mm arranged 2-up-1-down, recess depth ~5mm, cylindrical pin dia 1.8mm centered in each lobe.

findings.feature: USB-A ports x2 — vertical orientation
findings.evidence: Photo: two portrait rectangles ~38px wide x 75px tall = ~5.8 x 11.5mm at 0.600W and 0.666W (drawing 0.606/0.671), pitch ~0.066W = ~13mm; visible black tongue inside each.
findings.fix_hint: Two vertical rectangular cutouts 5.7 x 13mm (corner radius ~0.6mm), centers at x=118mm and x=131mm on the port line; add internal connector tongue offset to one side, both same orientation.

findings.feature: Ethernet RJ45 cutout
findings.evidence: Photo: near-square dark cutout at 0.379W (drawing 0.378W), measured ~74x72px = ~11 x 11mm — but a real RJ45 face needs ~15-16mm; the photo reading is the inner recessed opening, drawing shows a wider outline with top latch-notch profile.
findings.fix_hint: Cutout at x=74.5mm on port line: 16 x 13.5mm opening with RJ45 latch notch at top, jack face recessed ~1.5mm; verify against a macro ref before locking width (photo underreads it).

findings.feature: Base undercut + bottom intake ring (visible in rear elevation)
findings.evidence: Drawing: bottom corners run diagonally inward ~24mm horizontally while dropping ~8mm to a base ~150mm wide (0.77W). Photo: dark perforated band below the face, x 0.09W-0.91W (~162mm visible chord), ~13mm tall at center, curving up toward the ends — the perforated underside intake wrapping the raised circular base.
findings.fix_hint: Underside curves inward from the face bottom edge to a circular pedestal base dia ~150-155mm, 8mm tall; perforate the curved underside band with the same 1.6mm/3.0mm-pitch hex pattern; add rubber foot ring on the pedestal.

findings.feature: Power button
findings.evidence: Photo: flush circle dia ~52px = ~8mm at 0.903W on the port centerline, power glyph printed on it; drawing: outline circle dia ~7.5mm at 0.915W — rightmost element, clearly separated from the jack by ~14mm.
findings.fix_hint: Flush circular button dia 8mm, center x=178mm on the port line, proud of face by <=0.3mm with ~0.3mm perimeter gap ring; power glyph is print, not relief.

findings.feature: HDMI port
findings.evidence: Photo: the only landscape port — ~78px wide x 33px tall = ~12 x 5mm at 0.748W (drawing 0.757W), with tapered lower corners of HDMI type-A.
findings.fix_hint: HDMI type-A cutout ~14.5 x 5mm with 45-deg lower-corner tapers (classic D-shape), center x=148mm on the port line, connector tongue inside.

findings.feature: Corner wraps / vertical edge plan radius
findings.evidence: Photo: silhouette highlight band ~50-75px (~8-11mm projected) on each side before the flat; vent columns compress to ~0.7x pitch at field ends, meaning the vent edge already sits ~45 deg around the corner — so the plan radius is substantially larger than the projected band, est 30-40mm. Neither ref gives a top view to pin it.
findings.fix_hint: Give the chassis a plan corner radius ~35mm (verify against a top-view ref before locking); ensure the vent field's outer 2-3 hole columns lie on the curved region, flat face spanning roughly the central 155-175mm.

findings.feature: Port glyph / print zone
findings.evidence: Photo: printed symbols ~80px = ~12mm above the port centerline: lightning bolt centered over the TB group (x~0.22W), <...> over ethernet, USB trident over the USB-A pair, the text HDMI over HDMI, headphone glyph over the jack; none over the AC inlet.
findings.fix_hint: Flat print (no relief) 12mm above port centerline: 5 glyphs at x = 44/76/124/148/165mm; glyph height ~3mm; power glyph printed on the button itself.

findings.feature: Top edge radius (top face to rear face blend)
findings.evidence: Photo: narrow continuous highlight band ~15-20px = ~2.3-3mm along the top silhouette; drawing shows a single crisp line (radius below its line weight).
findings.fix_hint: Blend top face to rear face with a ~3mm radius, constant along the full width, growing into the 3D corner blend at each end.

findings.feature: Regulatory text zone on rear
findings.evidence: Confirmed ABSENT on the rear in both refs — the 3840px photo shows a clean face with only port glyphs; no regulatory block. Apple puts regulatory text on the underside of the base, not the rear.
findings.fix_hint: Do not add any regulatory text to the rear face; if the underside is ever modeled, the regulatory print ring belongs there.

grades.why: Confirmed: no rear geometry exists and no render frames were supplied — the face has never been modeled or rendered. Every cataloged feature is status=missing by definition.

Two independent refs cross-validate all 11 port-row x-positions to within 0.002-0.009 of body width and anchor scale to the known 197mm width, so placement, counts, order, and the defining vertical-port orientation are buildable to ~1mm. Docked 3 points: no depth information (recess depths inferred from connector standards), plan corner radius only bracketed at 30-40mm (needs a top view), the RJ45 cutout width is ambiguous (photo underreads vs standard), and the drawing crop lost its dimension callouts and most of the side view.

notes: Reference quality: apple_back.jpg is a near-orthographic 3840x2160 press image — excellent for x/y placement, useless for depths; dim_back-side.svg.png is a dimensions-style elevation whose numeric callouts are cropped out and whose side view is truncated at the canvas edge, so it contributes cross-validation only. Missing angles that would harden the spec: a top view (to pin the ~30-40mm plan corner radius and where the vent field tangents onto it), a raking 3/4 rear (port recess depths, hole entry chamfers, button proudness), and an underside shot (base diameter, foot ring, regulatory print). Two traps for the modeler explicitly caught: (1) all four Thunderbolt ports AND both USB-A ports are vertically oriented — both refs agree; (2) the port order is TBx4, ethernet, AC inlet (dead center at 0.50W), USB-Ax2, HDMI, jack, power button — not the power-first order assumed in the task focus. Hole-size estimate carries +/-0.3mm uncertainty at this photo resolution; Apple's 'over 4,000 perforations' claim covers rear + base combined, with ~1,100-1,200 on the rear field by row/column count.

findings.feature: End tabs (thin flat champagne strips at both long-axis ends of the front face)
findings.evidence: Refs (sth_front-2, cl_front-foam): flat coplanar tabs at each end, ~35px of 903px face width (~0.039, ~5-6mm), full face height, crisp inner edge where foam butts them, near-sharp outer corner. Render: no tab at either end — foam runs raggedly to a large-radius rounded bare-metal vertical corner (~65px at 20.6px/mm ≈ 3mm radius) that wraps to the side face.
findings.fix_hint: Add a flat tab strip at each end: 5.5mm wide, full face height, coplanar with the foam face (0-0.5mm proud), inner edge crisp with foam butting it; reduce the front-to-side corner radius to ~1mm passing through the tab.

findings.feature: Top lip strip along the front face
findings.evidence: Ref top-edge crop: foam meets the top edge of the front face directly — zero metal between the dark top plate and the foam. Render: a continuous smooth champagne strip (~30px ≈ 1.5mm tall) runs the full width between the top face and the foam.
findings.fix_hint: Delete the full-width top lip entirely; extend the foam field up so its cut face terminates at the top-face front edge.

findings.feature: Foam cell scale / density
findings.evidence: Normalized to the 150mm face width: ref dark-pore density 66/cm2, median pore dia 0.62mm, p90 0.94mm; render 14/cm2, median 0.95mm, p90 2.64mm — cells ~2.2x too coarse linearly, largest voids ~2.8x. Autocorrelation characteristic scale: ref 0.83mm vs render 3.25mm (render partly DOF-inflated, but the in-focus detail frame confirms thick gnarled struts and multi-mm cavities).
findings.fix_hint: Rescale the foam cell structure to ~0.45x current size: target 1-1.5mm cells, ~60-70 visible pore openings per cm2, strut thickness ~0.3-0.5mm; cap void depth so no through-black cavities larger than ~1mm read on the face.

findings.feature: Plateau relief vs foam (flush vs proud)
findings.evidence: Refs: plateau faces sit flush with the foam crest (≤~1mm proud); foam butts the plateau perimeter at the same plane with only a hairline shadow reveal. Render: plateaus are raised pads with a visible extruded side wall and hard drop shadows onto foam; the foam plane sits ~20-30px (≈1-1.5mm+) behind the plateau face and reads deeper due to void depth.
findings.fix_hint: Drop plateau faces to within 0.5mm of the foam crest and raise the foam cut plane so it is coplanar; remove the visible plateau side wall; keep only a ~0.3mm shadow reveal around the perimeter.

findings.feature: Pill slot cross-section (concave scoop)
findings.evidence: Ref (ref2_right_end zoom): smooth handle-scoop — broad soft entry fillet (widest on the inner shoulder), continuously dished bottom ~4-5mm deep, deepest at center, no steps. Render (front + detail frames): stepped counterbore — small uniform entry radius, vertical wall, a visible second inner rim/step, then a flat floor of constant depth.
findings.fix_hint: Remove the inner step; model the recess as one continuous concave dish: entry fillet R≈3-4mm blending into a smoothly curved bottom, max depth ~4.5mm at pill center, zero flat floor.

findings.feature: Pill slot proportions and offset within plateau
findings.evidence: Ref: pill width 0.34 of plateau width, height ~0.93 of plateau height (top/bottom rims only ~3-4%), outer rim ~0.04-0.07 of plateau width (pill hugs the outer edge). Render: width 0.41, height ~0.76 (fat ~12% rims top and bottom), outer margin 0.18-0.19. Offset direction and left/right mirroring are correct.
findings.fix_hint: Narrow the pill to 0.34 of plateau width, stretch it to 0.93 of plateau height (rims ~1mm top/bottom), and slide it outboard until the outer rim is ~1.5mm (~0.05 plateau width).

findings.feature: Plateau size and position from ends
findings.evidence: Ref: plateau 0.16 of face width, 0.76 of face height (foam lips ~0.12 above / ~0.09 below), outer edge 0.061 of face width from the end (tab + ~3.5mm foam gap), W:H 0.70. Render: 0.21 wide (+31%), 0.85 tall, outer edge only ~0.011 from the face end, W:H 0.79.
findings.fix_hint: Shrink plateaus to 24mm x 34.5mm (0.16 W x 0.76 H of a 150x45.5 face), keep the corner radius ratio, and move each outboard edge to 9mm from the device end so a ~3.5mm foam strip separates plateau from tab.

findings.feature: Foam-to-plateau boundary
findings.evidence: Ref: crisp boundary — foam cells cut cleanly against the plateau perimeter, no strands crossing it. Render: ragged foam fringe overhangs the plateau rims (top-left of left plateau, top edge of right plateau) and sparse scraggly strands with black gaps at the plateau-to-end gaps.
findings.fix_hint: Boolean-trim the foam flush to the plateau perimeter with zero overhang; fill the perimeter band so cell density at the boundary matches the mid-field (no see-through voids within 2mm of the plateau edge).

findings.feature: Foam field surface planarity / edge silhouette
findings.evidence: Ref: foam face is a planar cut — flat sparkle plane with tiny (<0.5mm) surface relief, clean straight silhouettes at top and ends. Render: foam surface billows with multi-mm relief and the silhouette at the ends/corners is ragged and fluffy with airy see-through voids.
findings.fix_hint: Flatten the foam front to a cut plane (clip strut relief to ±0.5mm) and make the field's outer silhouette straight lines matching the face edges.

findings.feature: NVIDIA engraving on left plateau
findings.evidence: Both sth refs: engraved 'NVIDIA' lettering (plus green eye badge) running vertically on the inner half of the left plateau only. Render: both plateaus are blank.
findings.fix_hint: Engrave vertical NVIDIA lettering ~0.1mm deep on the inner half of the left plateau, reading bottom-to-top, letter height ~4mm; leave the right plateau blank.

findings.feature: Plateau corner radius
findings.evidence: Ref r/plateau-width ≈ 0.16 (23px/145px); render ≈ 0.15 (95px/652px). Within measurement error.
findings.fix_hint: None — preserve r ≈ 0.16 of plateau width when resizing plateaus.

findings.feature: Pill slot stadium shape and orientation
findings.evidence: Both ref and render pills are vertical stadiums with full semicircular ends (end radius = half width), mirrored outboard on left and right plateaus.
findings.fix_hint: None — keep full-round ends when re-proportioning.

findings.feature: Front face aspect ratio
findings.evidence: Ref face 903x272px ≈ 3.32:1; render 3090x975px ≈ 3.17:1 — ~5% taller, within camera-pitch uncertainty between the two shots.
findings.fix_hint: None required; if chasing exactness, verify body H at 50.5mm incl. base vs 150mm width.

grades.why: Foam is present edge-to-edge in spirit, but an extra full-width top lip strip exists, foam wraps rounded corners instead of terminating at end tabs, plateau-to-end foam gaps are collapsed (0.011 vs 0.061 face width), and the foam plane sits behind the plateau plane instead of flush.

Count, portrait orientation, placement symmetry, and corner-radius ratio are right, but plateaus are 31% too wide (0.21 vs 0.16 face width), too tall (0.85 vs 0.76), pushed against the ends, and read as raised pads with side walls instead of flush inset islands.

Stadium shape, vertical orientation, and mirrored outboard offset are correct, but the pill is too wide (0.41 vs 0.34 plateau width), too short (0.76 vs 0.93 height), sits too far inboard (outer rim 0.18 vs ~0.05), and the section is a stepped flat-bottom counterbore instead of the ref's smooth concave scoop.
Feature absent: both ends lack the flat coplanar ~5.5mm champagne tab strips; the render substitutes a large rounded bare corner with ragged foam wrap.

Reads as foam, but cells are ~2.2x too coarse linearly (14 vs 66 pores/cm2, p90 pore 2.64 vs 0.94mm), voids too deep with see-through black cavities, surface billows instead of a planar cut, and boundaries against plateaus are ragged.

The parti (foam field + two gold islands + vertical pill grips) is recognizable and correctly mirrored, but missing end tabs, the extra top lip, proud plateaus, and 2x-coarse foam are all instantly flagged at arm's length; a machinist would reject on four separate features.

notes: Reference quality: sth_front-2 is near-orthographic and was used for all primary ratios (face 903px = 150mm, 6.02 px/mm); sth_front-1 adds a top-down oblique confirming foam meets the top edge; cl_front-foam (device on end) confirms tab width and the pill hugging the outer plateau edge — its dark 'window' is the mirror-polished plateau reflecting a dark room, not a geometric opening. Render front frame has depth-of-field blur in the foam mid-field which inflates autocorrelation scale (0.83mm ref vs 3.25mm render); the blob-density figure (66 vs 14 pores/cm2, ~2.2x linear) is the robust one, corroborated by the in-focus detail frame. Angles we lack: a true side/rear view and a raking-light front to pin exact plateau proudness in mm (I bounded it as 'flush ≤1mm ref vs several mm proud render' from shadow reads). Bottom edge treatment is close in both (foam over a recessed base line) — not flagged beyond corner rounding. Ratios carry roughly ±10% uncertainty from JPEG edge softness; all flagged deltas exceed that margin except plateau corner radius, which is passed as correct.

findings.feature: Pill intake cutouts: through-slot depth and diamond-mesh grille behind the plate
findings.evidence: cl_front-intake.jpg shows each pill is a TRUE through-cutout with a bright ~1mm machined chamfer rim and a black cross-hatch (diamond) mesh visible ~3-5mm behind the plate face, reading deep black in the front views. In the render both pills have a shallow, flat, light-tan floor at maybe 1-2mm depth — they read as blind engraved pockets, not intakes. No mesh geometry exists at all.
findings.fix_hint: Cut the pills fully through the plate (plate thickness ~2mm), add a crisp 45-degree chamfer ~0.8-1mm wide on the outer rim, and place a diamond/cross-hatch mesh plane ~3-4mm behind the plate face (pitch ~2mm, wire ~0.5mm) inside a dark cavity so the slot reads black from the front.

findings.feature: Pill lateral position within each plate (outboard offset, L/R mirror symmetry)
findings.evidence: Ref (storagereview, 1500px): left plate c=425, pill c=392 → pill offset 0.22 plate-widths OUTBOARD; right plate c=1084, pill c=1119 → also 0.22 outboard, perfectly mirrored. Render: left pill offset only ~0.12 plate-widths outboard, right pill ~0.04 (near-centered) — under-offset on both and asymmetric between left and right.
findings.fix_hint: Shift each pill outboard so the pill centerline sits ~0.28 plate-widths from the OUTER plate edge (offset from plate center = 0.22 plate-widths), and mirror the two plates exactly. This clears the wide blank field on the inboard side of the left plate where the logo sits on the real unit.

findings.feature: Plate and pill size relative to body / foam margins above and below plates
findings.evidence: Ref: plate width = 0.168 body-width, plate height = 0.76 face-height, foam margin above plate = 0.15 face-height, below = 0.095. Pill width = 0.073 body-width, pill aspect (h/w) = 3.1. Render: plate width = 0.203 body-width (+21%), plate height = 0.88 face-height, margins squeezed to ~0.06-0.07 both sides; pill width = 0.085 body-width, aspect 2.65 (pills ~17-20% too wide). Plates nearly touch the lid seam and base.
findings.fix_hint: Scale each plate to 0.168 x body width wide and 0.76 x front-face height tall; keep top foam margin ~0.15 face-height and bottom ~0.095 (asymmetric, plate rides low). Narrow the pills to 0.073 body-width keeping height, restoring h/w = 3.1.

findings.feature: Foam-to-side-rail and foam-to-lid seams at the front corners (plus stray embossed glyphs on left rail strip)
findings.evidence: Ref: the side panel wraps to the front with a tight rounded corner and the foam terminates against it in a crisp, straight vertical seam (visible as a clean ~0.045 body-width strip at each edge in storagereview; crisp seam in cl_front-intake). The foam-to-lid seam is a straight horizontal line. Render: the foam boundary at both upper corners is ragged and creeps over the corner rounding; the left edge strip carries faint vertical text/glyph-like embossed artifacts (y~560-940 region) that exist nowhere on the real unit; the right corner has foam bleeding to the extreme edge.
findings.fix_hint: Terminate the foam volume with clean planar trims: a straight vertical seam ~6-7mm from each front corner and a straight horizontal seam under the lid edge; delete/flatten whatever displacement or texture is stamping glyphs on the left rail strip.

findings.feature: Plate seating depth relative to foam crown
findings.evidence: In cl_front-intake the plate face sits essentially flush with the surrounding foam crown, foam lapping tight to the plate perimeter with no gap. In the render both plates stand visibly proud with a continuous dark shadow channel outlining the full perimeter, reading as glued-on tiles.
findings.fix_hint: Drop the plate faces to flush with the mean foam crown (within ±0.5mm) and close the perimeter gap so foam cells abut the plate edge directly; no uniform outline channel.

findings.feature: Smooth gold chin band across the bottom of the front face
findings.evidence: Render shows a clean smooth band ~0.075 face-heights tall running full-width under the foam. In both front refs the foam runs to within ~2-3% of the bottom edge and anything below is a recessed dark base in shadow — no bright smooth chin strip is visible on the front plane.
findings.fix_hint: Extend the foam's lower edge down to within ~1.5mm of the base plane and recess the base/chin behind the front plane so it reads as shadow, not as a bright band.

findings.feature: Top lid inset panel with perimeter groove and tick/vent row (visible in this frame)
findings.evidence: The render's top face has a large inset rectangular panel bounded by a machined groove, with a row of fine tick slots along its front edge. Reference close-up (cl_front-intake) shows the real lid is a single smooth continuous surface with only edge chamfers — no inset, groove, or tick row. Outside the strict front focus but plainly visible in the audited frame.
findings.fix_hint: Remove the inset panel, perimeter groove, and tick row from the top lid; model it as one smooth surface with the edge radius only.

findings.feature: Logo plate island: presence, count, and station (deliberately blank face)
findings.evidence: Ref has exactly two rounded-rect plate islands set into the foam near the front's outer ends, the left one carrying the (trademarked) logo in its inboard field. Render has exactly two plates at the correct stations, one pill each, with the left plate's inboard field left blank as specified — the plate-shaped island exists where it should.
findings.fix_hint: None for presence/count; apply the size/offset/flush corrections listed separately.

findings.feature: Plate corner radii and pill end radii
findings.evidence: Ref plate corner radius ~0.10 of plate width; render ~0.09 — within measurement error. Pill ends are full-radius stadium caps in both ref and render.
findings.fix_hint: None; preserve current radii through the rescale (radius should scale with the plate).

grades.why: The plate islands exist at the right stations with correct count, corner radii, and the intended blank face, but the plates are ~20% oversized with foam margins halved, the pills are ~20% too wide, under-offset outboard (and asymmetric L/R), sit proud instead of flush, and the pills are shallow blind pockets instead of through-slots.

The defining fine features are absent or wrong: no through-slot chamfer-and-mesh intake geometry, ragged foam terminations at both upper corners instead of crisp planar seams, stray glyph artifacts embossed on the left rail strip, an extra bright chin band below the foam, and an extra groove-and-tick inset on the lid.

The macro read (foam field flanked by two pill-bearing plates) is recognizably Spark, and nothing major is missing at arm's length; but nearly every secondary dimension is 15-25% off, the intake slots do not read as intakes, and the seam discipline collapses at the corners — a machinist would reject it on first close inspection.

notes: Reference-quality caveats: (1) storagereview_front.jpg and dgx-spark-front-ref.jpg are the same photograph, so effectively three unique refs. (2) cl_front-lower-logo.jpg actually shows the REAR port band (power-button stadium, 4x USB-C, HDMI, RJ45, dual QSFP) — it contains no front lower-logo plate; I judged the left NVIDIA plate (lower-left front zone, per the two true front refs) as the logo plate in question. No supplied ref shows any additional lower plate on the front, and the render correctly has none. (3) The only render frame is a 3/4-from-above press angle with no orthographic front, so vertical ratios (plate/pill heights, margins) carry ~5-8% perspective error; width ratios are reliable. An orthographic front render at matching focal length would let the plate-margin and pill-offset numbers be verified to ~1%. Foam microstructure was treated as material/displacement and not graded except where its boundary geometry (seam lines, corner wrap) is wrong.

findings.feature: Side-face edge radii (vertical corner columns and top-edge fillet)
findings.evidence: Ref cl_side-matte: corner radius ~12 px on 228 px body height = 0.05 of height (~2.5-3 mm); top edge reads crisp, ~1-1.5 mm. Render: corner wrap bands ~60-80 px on 525 px height = 0.12-0.15 (~6-8 mm), top-edge highlight band ~30-40 px wide implying a 3-4 mm fillet. The slab reads pillowed/soap-bar instead of the crisp extrusion in every reference.
findings.fix_hint: Reduce all four vertical corner radii to 2.5-3 mm and the top-edge fillet to 1-1.5 mm. Bottom edge fillet similar. Radii are currently ~2.5x oversize.

findings.feature: Recessed plinth foot under the body
findings.evidence: Ref cl_side-matte and sth_side-2 both show a dark recessed rounded plinth inset ~17% of depth (~25 mm) from each end, lifting the body ~2-2.5 mm (foot band ~10-15 px vs ~230-280 px body) with a visible shadow gap and body overhang. Render: bottom edge radius contacts the ground plane directly, no gap, no foot, no overhang line.
findings.fix_hint: Add a rounded-rectangle plinth foot: inset ~22-25 mm from front/back edges (and matching inset on the hidden axis), height 2.5 mm, dark recessed finish, so the body floats with a 2.5 mm shadow gap in side view.

findings.feature: Top raised plateau (central lid) and perimeter recess channel
findings.evidence: Ref cl_side-profile: central rounded-square plateau spans 404/543 = 0.74 of top width, corner radius 60/404 = 0.15 of plateau width, raised proud with a soft concave valley around it — from any elevated side view this relief defines the top read. Render: top is a single flat plane with an engraved scribe/groove outline whose near corner radius measures ~0.04 of panel span; no raised relief, no valley.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the scribed groove with real relief: plateau footprint ~110 x 110 mm on the 150 mm top, raised 1.5-2 mm, corner radius 16-18 mm, blended into a smooth concave channel ~6-8 mm wide around it.

findings.feature: Top slot near vent edge
findings.evidence: Ref cl_side-profile: one clean thin slit ~2-3 mm tall (10/552 px) spanning ~0.87 of top width, uniform dark, no internal texture. Render: the far-edge feature is a wide dark band with visible perforation/grille texture roughly 3-4x the slit height.
findings.fix_hint: Model as a single clean slit: 2.5 mm tall, ~130 mm long, square ends with small radii, dark interior, no perforation pattern. Verify it sits adjacent to the correct (front) foam edge.

findings.feature: Foam containment at front/back faces (left end silhouette)
findings.evidence: Ref sth_side-1-vertical: foam face is flush with adjacent metal to within ~1-2 px of 180 px body width (under 0.5 mm proud); cl_side-matte shows no foam breaking the side silhouette at all. Render: foam lumps protrude past the left silhouette by an equivalent ~2-4 mm along the full edge.
findings.fix_hint: Clamp foam geometry to the front/back face plane: max protrusion 0.5 mm beyond the metal rim, none beyond the side-face silhouette. Reduce cell/lump scale so individual blobs are under ~1.5 mm.

findings.feature: Seam between top shell and body on the side face
findings.evidence: All three side refs show one continuous metal wrap — no parting line anywhere between top and side. Render shows a hard break running exactly along the top-edge fillet tangent where gold meets grey, reading as a two-piece clamshell seam. Cannot fully exclude a material-assignment boundary, but the line follows a geometric tangent, suggesting a modeled edge split.
findings.fix_hint: Remove any edge split/crease at the top-edge fillet tangent; the side-to-top transition must be a single continuous fillet with no coincident geometry boundary.

findings.feature: Side face flatness and cleanliness (no ports, vents, reliefs)
findings.evidence: Ref side face is dead flat and featureless; render side face is likewise smooth with no ports, vents, or reliefs. Minor caveat: the very wide corner blends eat into the flat area, and the highlight spread hints at slight barrel softness, but no discrete feature errors.
findings.fix_hint: No feature change; flatness will read correctly once corner radii are tightened per the radii finding.

findings.feature: Side profile proportion (depth 150 x height 50.5, ratio 2.97)
findings.evidence: Render mid-height: ~1605 px long x ~525 px tall = 3.06, vs spec 2.97 and refs 3.08-3.23 (lens-inflated). Within ~3%, inside measurement noise for this perspective camera.
findings.fix_hint: None required; re-verify with a level, long-lens frame matching cl_side-matte since the elevated perspective camera limits precision to ~3-4%.

grades.why: Measured 3.06 vs spec 2.97 — within perspective measurement noise, so the footprint reads right. Held below 8 because the elevated, perspective render camera prevents a true elevation check against cl_side-matte, and the oversized radii visually shorten the flat face.

Every measurable radius is ~2-2.5x oversize (0.12-0.15 of height vs 0.05 in ref; top fillet 3-4 mm vs ~1 mm). This changes the object's fundamental read from crisp machined slab to pillowed soap bar — the single most identity-damaging error in this view.

The side face itself is correctly seamless — no spurious panel lines. Docked for the hard break along the top-edge tangent that reads as a clamshell parting line absent on the real unit; uncertainty whether it is geometry or a material boundary keeps this from scoring lower.

Flat, featureless matte face matches the references — no invented ports or vents. Points lost for foam lumps breaking the left silhouette and the corner blends encroaching on what should be flat area.

Proportion and face cleanliness are close, but three severity-4 misses stack in this one view: soap-bar radii, a completely absent plinth foot (body sits on the floor), and a flat-scribed top where the ref has raised plateau relief. A machinist would separate render from reference in seconds.

notes: Reference-quality caveats: (1) No dead-level raking-light side shot exists to pin micro-radii below ~1 mm precision; cl_side-matte is closest but lens-widened (ratio 3.23 vs spec 2.97), so all radius ratios were normalized against body height, not width. (2) The render camera is elevated with heavy DOF and perspective — the left foam end and both corner radii are blurred, limiting radius measurement to about +/-20%; next audit loop should get a level, long-lens, low-DOF frame matching cl_side-matte framing. (3) Cannot confirm from the provided refs which top edge (front vs back) carries the thin slit — cl_side-profile does not disambiguate orientation; verify against a labeled front photo before repositioning it. (4) The foot inset (~17% of depth per end) is read through shadow in both side refs and could be 15-25 mm; a bottom or three-quarter-low reference would nail it. Judged geometry only; the grey-vs-gold top tone difference was ignored except where the boundary line itself implies a modeled seam.

findings.feature: Recess transition (border-to-panel dish slope)
findings.evidence: Ref (cl_side-profile): the border descends into the panel via a wide smooth dished slope ~55px on a 540px face = 10% of face width (~15mm annulus) — this is what produces the signature halo band and makes the panel read clearly recessed. Render (q34 + top): the transition is a narrow steep step ~25-35px on a ~3300px face = ~1% wide, reading as an engraved outline; in the q34 frame the top face reads essentially FLAT with a scored rectangle, not dished.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the step wall with a tangent-continuous dish: slope annulus ~15mm wide, recess depth ~1.2-1.5mm, G2 blend at both top and bottom of the slope. Then add the ref's fine perimeter groove (~2mm wide, ~0.5mm deep) right at the panel boundary.

findings.feature: Corrugated 'zipper' ribbing on the recess wall
findings.evidence: Render top + q34: the entire recess boundary wall carries regular rectangular teeth (period ~22px in the top close-up, matching the weave displacement pitch), reading as a ratchet/tank-tread line — visible as a dotted chain even in the full q34 frame. Ref: the boundary is a perfectly smooth groove line; zero ribbing anywhere on the transition.
findings.fix_hint: Mask the weave displacement/normal map OFF the recess wall and border; the wall/groove must be geometrically smooth. Count of ribs should be zero.

findings.feature: Weave pattern pitch
findings.evidence: Ref: diagonal satin bands, period ~17-20px on a 540px face = 3.2-3.7% of face width (~5mm on the 150mm face), plainly visible at product-photo distance. Render: fine corduroy threads at 8-9px on a ~6000px-wide face in the top view = ~0.15% of face width (~0.25mm) — roughly 20x too fine; the weave is invisible in the q34 frame where the ref shows it clearly. Direction ('/', rising to the right) matches.
findings.fix_hint: Rebuild the weave at ~5mm period (perpendicular pitch ~3.5mm), 45 degrees, '/' direction, as soft low-contrast satin bands (wide sinusoidal relief), not fine threads.

findings.feature: Recess/panel corner radius
findings.evidence: Ref: panel corner radius ~65-70px on a 400px panel = ~17% of panel width (~19mm), with a continuous-curvature squircle character. Render q34: loop corner radius ~80px on a ~2280px loop = ~3.5% of panel width (~4-6mm) — a 4-5x too-tight fillet between long straights; the corner near (640,860) in the q34 frame turns over ~80px total.
findings.fix_hint: Increase panel corner radius to ~18-19mm (0.17 of panel width) and use a superellipse/G2 corner, not a circular fillet between straights.

findings.feature: Thin vent slot on the top face (near foam edge)
findings.evidence: Ref (cl_side-profile): a slot 485px long on the 540px face (90% of face width, ~133mm), ~11px tall (2%, ~3mm), full-radius ends, its centerline ~56px (10%, ~15mm) from the face edge, sitting in the border between panel and edge and extending ~40px (7%) wider than the panel on each side. Render: no slot exists anywhere on the top face in either frame (checked q34 full and the top-view front-rim crop).
findings.fix_hint: Add a 133 x 3mm stadium slot (full-radius ends), centered on the face width, centerline 15mm from the foam-side face edge, with a small chamfer on its lower lip (the ref shows a bright lower-lip light catch).

findings.feature: Panel size and border widths
findings.evidence: Ref: panel is 74% of face width x 67% of face depth — side borders 68/70px (12.6%, ~19mm), front/back borders 92/88px (~16.5%, ~25mm); the panel is rectangular (1.10:1), wider than deep. Render: edge-to-recess border reads ~7-8% of face width all around (rim ~2.5-3% + flat dark band ~4-5%), so the panel is ~85% of the face and square-ish — borders roughly half the reference width, panel ~10-15% oversized per axis.
findings.fix_hint: Shrink the panel to ~111 x 101mm on the 150mm face, centered; side borders 19mm, front/back borders 25mm (the extra front width is what houses the slot).

findings.feature: Weave coverage area
findings.evidence: Ref: texture is confined strictly to the recessed panel; the border, seam zone, and dish slope are smooth satin metal. Render top view: the fine weave covers the ENTIRE dark top surface — border, slope, and panel alike (stripes visible on both sides of the recess boundary in the recess-corner crop).
findings.fix_hint: Clip the weave texture to the panel interior only — everything outside the panel groove line is untextured.

findings.feature: Outer seam line / rim expression
findings.evidence: Ref: a hairline scored seam at 14px = 2.6% (~4mm) inset from the face edges, with ~3mm corner radii; surfaces on both sides are co-planar and the same continuous face. Render: this line is expressed as a hard rim/inlay break — the dark top plate sits visibly below/inside a proud gold rim (~2.5-3% wide) with a step highlight, giving a picture-frame read the ref does not have. Rim width matches the ref seam inset, but the character is a level/material break instead of a flush score.
findings.fix_hint: Make the border co-planar with the outer rim (zero step at the seam); model the seam as a ~0.3mm-wide, ~0.2mm-deep score line 4mm in from each edge, corners r~3mm.

findings.feature: Foam-to-frame flushness at the top front edge (3/4 silhouette)
findings.evidence: Ref hero_3q: the foam face reads flush with the frame — the top edge fillet transitions directly into foam texture with at most a hairline reveal. Render q34: the foam is recessed well behind the front plane with a deep gold return/ledge under the top front edge (~25-40px on the 3300px face, plus a shadow gap), creating a stepped ledge silhouette the ref does not show.
findings.fix_hint: Pull the foam surface forward to within ~1mm of the frame front plane; reduce the gold return under the top edge to a ~1mm reveal.

findings.feature: Corner bracket stadium cutout proportions (visible in 3/4)
findings.evidence: Ref hero: the stadium cutout nearly fills the bracket plate vertically (~0.85-0.88 of plate height; oval/plate width ~0.46). Render q34: oval height 329px on a 571px plate = 0.58 — top and bottom margins roughly 3x too fat; width ratio 0.40 is close. Render plates also sit proud of the foam and show foam outboard of the plate at the corner, where the ref plate reads flush at the corner edge.
findings.fix_hint: Lengthen the stadium cutout to ~0.85 of plate height (keep width); reduce plate proud-ness and move the plate to terminate at the corner edge.

findings.feature: Outer edge fillets (top-to-side and vertical corners)
findings.evidence: Render q34: top-edge highlight band ~50-60px on a ~1850-2000px face = ~3% (~4.5mm); vertical back-right corner turns over ~80px = ~4.3% (~6.5mm). Ref hero: vertical corner gold band ~7px on a 205px face = 3.4% (~5mm); cl profile edge strip 12px/540 = 2.2% (~3.3mm, straight-on view underreads a fillet). Render is within ~1-1.5mm of the ref read — inside the measurement error of these references.
findings.fix_hint: No change required; if anything, verify the vertical corners are not above ~6mm — they read at the top of the tolerance band.

findings.feature: Overall 3/4 proportion (height:width)
findings.evidence: Render q34 far-end front-face height/width = ~0.37 vs ref hero ~0.356 (true 50.5/150 = 0.337); the near end reads 0.44 but that is wide-angle proximity distortion — the render camera is much closer than the ref's telephoto. Box read, footprint squareness, and stance all match.
findings.fix_hint: No geometry change; for future overlay passes render with a longer lens (~85mm equiv) to match press-photo perspective before judging proportion.

grades.why: The recess exists conceptually but every defining parameter is off: step wall instead of a 15mm dished slope (10x too narrow), corner radius 4-5x too tight, panel ~10-15% oversized with half-width borders, square instead of 1.10:1, ribbed wall artifact, and the 133x3mm front slot is missing entirely. In the 3/4 frame the top reads flat-engraved, not dished.

Direction ('/') is right and angle is plausible, but the pitch is ~20x too fine (0.15% of face width vs 3.5% in ref), so the pattern is invisible at any distance where the ref shows it; it also spills over border and slope (ref confines it to the panel) and its displacement corrugates the recess wall.

Border zone is flat (good) and the rim width coincidentally matches the ref's 4mm seam inset, but the seam is expressed as a proud-rim/sunken-inlay step instead of a flush hairline score, the borders are ~half the reference width (7-8% vs 12.6/16.5%), and the slot that lives in the front border is absent.

Top-edge and vertical-corner fillets measure ~4.5-6.5mm vs a ~4-6mm reference read — within the error bars of a blurry hero photo and a straight-on profile; vertical corners sit at the top of tolerance. No face-to-face transition errors found.

Silhouette, stance, and fillets carry the object, but the top face — the focus of this audit — fails its defining read: no dish/halo, wrong panel size and radius, invisible weave, ribbed boundary artifact, and a missing slot. A machinist would separate render from reference at a glance from any top-visible angle.

notes: Reference quality caveats: only two refs were supplied for this pass. cl_side-profile.jpg (1200x800) is a straight-on shot of the top face standing on edge — excellent for in-plane ratios (all border/panel/slot/radius percentages above come from it at 540px face width, ~0.28mm/px) but recess DEPTH is inferred from shading only, so the 1.2-1.5mm depth in the fix hints is an estimate; treat the 15mm slope width as the hard constraint. nv_hero_3q.png shows the device at only ~205px wide and blurry, so hero-derived numbers (fillets, bracket margins, foam flushness) carry roughly +/-30% error. There is no clean high-res true-3/4 reference (consistent with the repo note that the DGX Spark reference ceiling is 3/4 press only), so far-side face transitions and the rear are unaudited. The render q34 camera is close/wide-angle versus the ref telephoto — proportion judgments were made at the far end of the box to minimize lens bias. Weave angle in the render could not be measured precisely under perspective (direction verified as matching; angle unverified). Which foam edge (front vs rear) the top slot abuts cannot be determined from these two refs — the slot's 15mm offset should be placed on the same side as whichever face the repo's other refs (cl_front-*, nv_rear-panel) show it adjacent to; other refs exist in render/ref/dgx-spark/ but were outside this audit's provided set. The ribbed-wall artifact strongly suggests the weave is implemented as displacement that is not masked off the recess wall — fixing the weave mask (finding 7) may resolve the zipper artifact (finding 2) in the same change.

findings.feature: REAR: full-face open-cell foam field between champagne rails
findings.evidence: sth_rear-2: entire rear face between two solid champagne vertical rails is the same open-cell metal foam as the front, full height, with only a thin champagne lip at top/bottom. build_scene.py build_dgx_spark docstring: 'Rear skipped.' — rear is currently a bare champagne rounded_box face.
findings.fix_hint: Clone the front FOAM3D treatment onto the rear (+y) face: foam field ~137 x 46 mm (device 150 x 50.5), bounded by ~6-7 mm solid champagne rails at both x ends and ~2.5 mm champagne lips top/bottom (reuse foam_lip 2.53). Foam must flow around the recessed port panel, not stop at a straight border.

findings.feature: REAR: recessed polished port-panel bar
findings.evidence: sth_rear-2 pixel read (5.35 px/mm): panel spans 126 mm of the 150 mm width (inset ~11.5 mm each side), height ~15-17 mm, bottom edge ~6-7 mm above base. nv_rear-panel diagram agrees: 127.7 x 15.4-16.9 mm, insets 11.0/11.3 mm. cl_rear-overview macro shows foam overhanging the panel with a shadow line = panel recessed below foam surface. Nothing modeled.
findings.fix_hint: Cut a rounded-rect pocket in the rear foam: 127 x 16 mm, corner r ~6-7.5 mm (near-stadium ends), left edge 11.5 mm from device left edge, panel z-span ~6.5-22.5 mm (center z 14 mm above base), floor recessed 1.5-2.5 mm below foam surface, polished champagne floor material.

findings.feature: BOTTOM: large rounded-square access plate
findings.evidence: sth_bottom (3.58 px/mm): plate 402 x 377 px = 112.3 x 105.3 mm, side margins 18.2/19.0 mm, rear margin 22.3 mm, front margin 23.2 mm — essentially centered. Corner radius reads ~23-26 mm (far rounder than the top panel's ~8 mm). cl_bottom-plate macro shows a ~0.6-1.0 mm seam groove around it, plate flush to <=0.5 mm recessed. Nothing modeled — underside is the untouched flat of rounded_box.
findings.fix_hint: Add a 112 x 105 mm plate (long axis parallel to the rear edge, matching the top panel's 114x105 orientation), corner r 24 +/- 3 mm, centered in x, biased ~0.5 mm toward rear, delimited by a 0.8 mm wide x ~0.5 mm deep seam groove; plate face flush with the bottom.

findings.feature: REAR: 4x vertical USB-C ports (port 1 = power in)
findings.evidence: Diagram + sth_rear-2 agree: centers at 0.100/0.158/0.216/0.273 of panel width = 12.7/20.0/27.4/34.7 mm from panel left edge, pitch 7.35 mm. cl_rear-overview macro: each is a vertical stadium countersink ~3.5 x 8.8 mm holding a 2.6 x 8.4 mm black opening with a centered tongue blade ~0.7 mm thick recessed ~1.5 mm. All centered on panel mid-height (z ~14 mm).
findings.fix_hint: Four vertical stadium cuts at x = 12.7/20.0/27.4/34.7 mm from panel-left, pitch 7.35 mm: outer countersink 3.5 x 8.8 (r = w/2), inner opening 2.62 x 8.47 (reuse the settled front USB-C dims), dark interior + tongue blade per the Studio front-port recipe.

findings.feature: REAR: twin QSFP cage in a single wide cutout
findings.evidence: Diagram: outer cutout spans 0.667-0.963 of panel width = 84.7-122.2 mm from panel-left (37.5 mm wide x ~9.5 mm tall) containing two ~17.5 x 8.5 mm cage openings at 19.0 mm pitch with ~1 mm center divider. sth_rear-2 confirms one wide dark rectangle with a mid divider dominating the right third of the panel. Nothing modeled.
findings.fix_hint: One 37.5 x 9.5 mm rectangular recess (corner r ~1 mm, thin ~1 mm raised bezel lip), left edge 84.7 mm from panel-left, vertically centered on panel; inside, two 18.35 x 8.5 mm dark cage openings at centers 94.2 and 113.2 mm (19 mm pitch), 1 mm divider wall.

findings.feature: BOTTOM: rear intake slot with mesh screen
findings.evidence: sth_bottom: slot 478 px = 133.5 mm long x ~3.9 mm wide, ends inset ~8 mm from each side edge, near edge 12.0 mm / centerline 14.0 mm from the rear edge of the bottom face, full-radius rounded ends. cl_bottom-plate macro shows a fine diamond/expanded mesh recessed ~1-1.5 mm inside the slot. Not modeled, never rendered.
findings.fix_hint: Cut a 133.5 x 3.9 mm stadium slot (end r = 1.95 mm) in the bottom face, centerline 14 mm forward of the rear edge, centered in x; inset a diamond-mesh plane (cell pitch ~0.9 mm) 1.2 mm below the surface; slot inner walls with ~0.3 mm edge break.

findings.feature: REAR: HDMI Type-A opening
findings.evidence: Diagram center at 0.402 of panel width = 50.9 mm from panel-left; photo agrees (0.397). Opening ~14.7 x 5.6 mm (matches real HDMI-A 15 x 6). cl_rear-overview macro: wide edge UP with the pin row at top, inward-tapered/chamfered lower corners; vertically centered on the panel.
findings.fix_hint: HDMI-A cutout 15 x 6 mm centered at 50.9 mm from panel-left, z-centered; top edge straight, bottom corners chamfered inward ~45 deg over ~2 mm (classic HDMI trapezoid), dark recessed interior ~4 mm deep with a thin top contact-tongue.

findings.feature: REAR: RJ45 jack
findings.evidence: Diagram + photo: center at 0.563-0.567 of panel width = 71.7 mm from panel-left. Diagram silhouette: ~13 mm wide x ~9.1 mm main body plus a ~2.2 mm tall clip-notch step on TOP center; tallest port on the panel, extends above HDMI's top line. Stylization risk: real modular jacks run ~15 x 13, diagram may compress height.
findings.fix_hint: RJ45 cutout ~14 wide x ~11.5 tall (main aperture) + top-center clip notch ~6 wide x 2.2 tall, center x = 71.7 mm from panel-left, z-centered on panel; verify height against a straight-on macro before final.

findings.feature: REAR: ON/OFF power button (flush engraved pill)
findings.evidence: Leftmost element: diagram center at 0.045 of panel width = 5.7 mm from panel-left; ~3.3 x 9.1 mm vertical stadium. cl_rear-overview macro confirms it is a flush champagne pill defined only by an engraved outline ring — NOT a dark hole; same height family as the USB-C ports, slightly narrower.
findings.fix_hint: Engrave a 3.3 x 9.1 mm vertical stadium outline (groove ~0.25 mm wide x 0.2 mm deep) centered 5.7 mm from panel-left, z-centered; face stays flush polished champagne, no dark interior.

findings.feature: REAR: champagne side rails + top/bottom lips framing the foam
findings.evidence: sth_rear-2: solid anodized vertical bars ~6-7 mm wide at both x-extremes of the rear face, full height, carrying the 6.09 mm body edge radius; thin champagne lip along top and bottom edges where the shell wraps over the foam.
findings.fix_hint: Bound the rear foam field with 6.5 mm solid champagne rails at x = +/-75 mm ends and ~2.5 mm lips at top/bottom (mirror of the front's ~1 mm rails is WRONG here — rear rails are visibly wider than the front's; use 6.5 mm).

findings.feature: BOTTOM: recessed foot / riser plinth
findings.evidence: sth_rear-2 and cl_bottom-intake show a thin dark gap/shadow band under the body implying the shell floats ~2-3 mm above the table on an inset riser, but no provided reference resolves the foot's actual shape, inset, or count. sth_bottom (straight-on) shows no discrete corner feet.
findings.fix_hint: Provisionally: one inset plinth ~2.5 mm tall inset ~8-10 mm from the footprint (Studio-foot recipe at Spark scale). Acquire a bottom-oblique or table-level macro before committing — flag in MEASUREMENTS.md as UNMEASURED.

findings.feature: BOTTOM: outer frame margins and edge treatment
findings.evidence: sth_bottom: flat champagne margin of ~18.6 mm (sides) and ~22.7 mm (front/rear) around the plate; cl_bottom-plate macro shows a crisp but eased ~1.5-2 mm radius where the bottom meets the side faces, and the bottom meets the front/rear FOAM faces directly (foam band visible wrapping the edge in sth_bottom top/bottom of frame).
findings.fix_hint: Keep the bottom face dead flat; ease bottom-to-side edges at r 1.5-2 mm; at front/rear edges terminate against the foam faces with the same 2.5 mm champagne lip used on the vertical faces.

findings.feature: Body envelope 150 x 150 x 50.5, edge R 6.09
findings.evidence: cl_bottom-intake caliper reads 149.92 mm across the depth — the modeled SPARK dict (width 150.0, depth 150.0, height 50.5, edge_R 6.09) matches the references. The only geometry these six views touch that already exists in the model.
findings.fix_hint: No change. The rear/bottom features above must be cut into this existing envelope without altering it.

findings.feature: REAR: DC power symbol under port 1
findings.evidence: cl_rear-overview macro + nv_rear-panel diagram: an IEC DC glyph (solid bar over dashed bar, ~3 x 2 mm) printed/engraved on the panel directly below the leftmost USB-C port, marking it as power-in.
findings.fix_hint: Engrave/decal a ~3 x 2 mm DC glyph (solid line above three short dashes) centered under port 1, ~1.5 mm below the port countersink. Check the D2 trademark gate — this is a regulatory glyph, not a logo, but confirm policy before adding.

findings.feature: REAR: micro-hole cluster in upper foam
findings.evidence: cl_rear-overview: a small cluster of ~5-7 round drilled holes (~1 mm dia) in the foam above the panel, roughly over the USB4-HDMI gap near the top of the face. Identity uncertain (LED light-pipe, mic, or pressure vent); not resolvable in sth_rear-2 at its resolution.
findings.fix_hint: Only add after a higher-res straight-on rear macro confirms position/count; provisionally ~6 holes of 1 mm dia in a tight ~5 x 3 mm cluster, center-x ~42-45 mm from panel-left, ~8 mm below the top edge.

findings.feature: BOTTOM: label / regulatory text zone
findings.evidence: No label, etch, or regulatory block is visible on the bottom plate in sth_bottom, cl_bottom-intake, or cl_bottom-plate (likely a prerelease unit). Retail units normally carry regulatory text on the plate.
findings.fix_hint: Leave the plate blank (consistent with the D2 no-trademark gate) unless a retail-unit bottom reference is acquired; if added later, it belongs centered on the access plate.

grades.why: Confirmed zero in source, not assumed: build_dgx_spark docstring in render/build_scene.py states 'Rear skipped.', and the SPARK dict has no rear-port, panel, or rear-foam parameters. The rear (+y) face is a bare champagne rounded_box face. Note the turnaround includes a rear34 camera tag, so any rear34 frame currently shows this bare face.

Confirmed zero: no bottom plate, intake slot, mesh, foot, or seam exists in the Spark builder — the underside is the untouched flat of rounded_box (the intake_band/foot code belongs to the Mac Studio builder only), and no bottom render exists in portraits or the turnaround tag set (front/q34/side/rear34).

Three mutually confirming sources (official NVIDIA diagram + full elevation + macro) let every port be placed to within ~1% of panel width with two-source agreement, and the macro resolves countersinks, tongue blades, recess shadow, and the flush button. Docked 2: panel recess depth and corner radius are estimated not measured, RJ45 height may be stylized in the diagram, and the micro-hole cluster is unresolved.
