Plate size/margins and slot size/position are solid pixel measurements with an internal cross-check (bottom plate 112x105 nearly matches the already-measured 114x105 top panel), the caliper pins the envelope, and the macro resolves the mesh and seam. Docked 3: foot/riser geometry is not resolvable in any provided view, plate corner radius carries +/-3 mm uncertainty, and recess depths are inferred from shadows only.

notes: Reference quality: strong for a first rear/bottom build pass — the NVIDIA line diagram (nv_rear-panel.png) and sth_rear-2 agree on all eight rear elements to ~1% of panel width, which is unusual luck; treat the diagram as position truth and the photos as shape truth. Coordinate convention used above: 'panel-left' = the left inner edge of the recessed rear panel, which sits 11.5 mm from the device's left edge; panel is 127 x 16 mm with center 14 mm above the base. Missing angles that block full closure: (1) a bottom-oblique or table-level shot to resolve the foot/riser — currently the single largest unknown; (2) a straight-on rear macro of the RJ45/QSFP right half (cl_rear-overview only covers button through HDMI) to confirm RJ45 aperture height and the QSFP bezel lip; (3) any shot resolving panel and plate recess DEPTHS (all depths above are shadow-inferred). The diagram's RJ45 height (9.1+2.2 mm) conflicts with standard modular-jack proportions (~13 mm tall) — do not trust it blindly. Everything here traces to pixel ratios per the method; recommend appending these numbers to render/MEASUREMENTS.md under a REAR/BOTTOM section before modeling so the fix loop grades against fixed targets.

findings.feature: Spark-to-Studio front width ratio (relative scale, left-right axis)
findings.evidence: Spark front face measures 906 px corner-to-corner ((1875,1315)->(2765,1450)) vs Studio 938 px ((300,1395)->(1230,1521)) — raw ratio 0.966. After removing yaw foreshortening (Studio ~21-24 deg, cos 0.93; Spark ~3-9 deg, cos 0.995) and correcting ~4% for the Spark sitting farther, the width ratio is ~0.94 vs the required 150/197 = 0.761. Meanwhile the HEIGHT ratio is exact (227/444 = 0.511 -> ~0.53 corrected vs 0.532 required) and the footprint DEPTH ratio is correct (top-face depth edges 496 vs 726 px -> 0.73-0.77 corrected vs ~0.74). 'Spark is nearer' is ruled out: at the proximity needed to justify 906 px its height would read ~293 px, not 227. Net: the Spark is ~20-24% oversized along its front (width) axis only, so its footprint reads ~180x150 mm instead of 150x150, and in the pair shot it reads nearly as wide as the Studio instead of 3/4 as wide.
findings.fix_hint: Scale the Spark model x0.80-0.81 along its local left-right (front-width) axis only, restoring width = depth = 150 mm; do NOT touch height (50.5 mm) or depth — both currently verify correct against the Studio. After the fix, re-measure: Spark front width should land at ~730-745 px at this camera, and the top slab should read as a square under the same projection as the Studio's top.

findings.feature: Spark gold end plates (handle plates) size relative to front face
findings.evidence: Render plates are ~205 px wide of a 906 px face = 0.225 of front width, and run the full face height (plate 1098->1330 vs face 1085->1315, ~100%). Reference sth_front-1.jpg: plate 108 px of 651 px device width = 0.166, and plate height 158 px of 200 px face = ~79% with visible foam margins above and below. Render plates are ~35% too wide relative to the face and are missing the top/bottom foam margins. Note: the width fix on the body preserves this ratio, so the plates need their own correction on top of it.
findings.fix_hint: Resize each plate to ~0.165 of front width (~25 mm wide on the 150 mm face) and ~0.79 of face height (~40 mm of 50.5 mm), leaving ~5-6 mm foam margin at top and bottom; keep plate positions flush at the left/right ends of the face.

findings.feature: Foam silhouette containment at base and side planes
findings.evidence: Foam speckle geometry spills outside the machined envelope: past the right side plane by up to ~40 px (~8 mm) around (2790-2820, 1250-1470), and hangs below/breaks the base contact line by +-10-15 px (~2-3 mm) along (1875,1315)->(2765,1450). Reference shows the foam trimmed flush: the device's bottom and side edges are clean straight lines. The fuzz reads as crumbling foam at pair-shot distance and degrades the ground-contact line.
findings.fix_hint: Clamp the foam displacement/particle geometry to the chassis volume: trim flush to the side planes and base plane with max overshoot 0.5 mm (~3 px at this scale); the base silhouette must be a straight line between the two bottom corners.

findings.feature: Pair yaw relationship (stance)
findings.evidence: Studio is yawed ~21-24 deg (side-face x-span 384 px past the near corner; base-edge slope 0.16), while the Spark is nearly frontal at ~3-9 deg (side sliver only ~45-50 px; slab depth-edge dx 140/45 px). Same rotation direction, but the 15 deg mismatch hides the Spark's 150 mm depth — it reads as a thin slab rather than a square-footprint box — and makes the pair stance look accidental rather than composed. Not a modeling error; a pose choice.
findings.fix_hint: Rotate the Spark to ~18-22 deg yaw, matching the Studio's, so its right side face and square footprint become legible; keep both front-left corners on the existing floor line.

findings.feature: Studio base perforated antenna ring band height
findings.evidence: Render: the dotted/dim band above floor contact spans only ~17-20 px of a 442 px front face (~4% of body height; columns x=700 dim from y~1433 to contact ~1452, x=900 from ~1468 to ~1486). Reference apple_front.jpg: ring band 115 px of 1258 px body = 9.1% (~8.6 mm). Even allowing for the ~33 deg downward camera compressing and partially self-occluding the under-curved ring (expected ~30 px visible), the render band is short, and its top boundary is a soft falloff rather than a crisp recess seam. Moderate confidence — some loss is legitimate occlusion.

notes: Reference-quality caveats: no pair reference exists, so all relative-scale checks are computed against real dims (197x197x95 vs 150x150x50.5 mm) via camera-consistent pixel ratios. The Spark reference is front-only; its top-face inset and side geometry are unverifiable this loop (repo notes already flag the Spark ref ceiling). The headline width finding (+20-24%) survives worst-case assumptions in that band — to force the measured width ratio to be correct, the Studio would need ~41 deg yaw, which would require ~810 px of visible side face vs the 384 px actually present. Measurement floor: foam edge raggedness gives +-8 px on Spark edges, +-3 px on Studio edges. Suggested fix order: (1) Spark width axis x0.81, (2) re-shoot this exact frame, re-run the same corner measurements, (3) then plates and foam trim — plate ratios are body-scale-invariant so they need their own pass regardless.
